constructors.name: PackedColorArray
constructors.summary: Constructs a [PackedColorArray] as a copy of the given [PackedColorArray].

constructors.name: PackedColorArray
constructors.summary: Constructs a new [PackedColorArray]. Optionally, you can pass in a generic [Array] that will be converted. [b]Note:[/b] When initializing a [PackedColorArray] with elements, it must be initialized with an [Array] of [Color] values: [codeblock] var array = PackedColorArray([Color(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), Color(0.4, 0.5, 0.6)]) [/codeblock]

members.name: Append
members.summary: Appends an element at the end of the array (alias of [method push_back]).

members.name: AppendArray
members.summary: Appends a [PackedColorArray] at the end of this array.

members.name: Bsearch
members.signature: public int Bsearch(Color value, bool before)
members.summary: Finds the index of an existing value (or the insertion index that maintains sorting order, if the value is not yet present in the array) using binary search. Optionally, a [param before] specifier can be passed. If [code]false[/code], the returned index comes after all existing entries of the value in the array. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method bsearch] on an unsorted array results in unexpected behavior.

members.name: Clear
members.summary: Clears the array. This is equivalent to using [method resize] with a size of [code]0[/code].

members.name: Count
members.summary: Returns the number of times an element is in the array.

members.name: Duplicate
members.summary: Creates a copy of the array, and returns it.

members.name: Erase
members.summary: Removes the first occurrence of a value from the array and returns [code]true[/code]. If the value does not exist in the array, nothing happens and [code]false[/code] is returned. To remove an element by index, use [method remove_at] instead.

members.name: Fill
members.summary: Assigns the given value to all elements in the array. This can typically be used together with [method resize] to create an array with a given size and initialized elements.

members.name: Find
members.signature: public int Find(Color value, int from)
members.summary: Searches the array for a value and returns its index or [code]-1[/code] if not found. Optionally, the initial search index can be passed.

members.name: Get
members.summary: Returns the [Color] at the given [param index] in the array. If [param index] is out-of-bounds or negative, this method fails and returns [code]Color(0, 0, 0, 1)[/code]. This method is similar (but not identical) to the [code][][/code] operator. Most notably, when this method fails, it doesn't pause project execution if run from the editor.

members.name: Has
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the array contains [param value].

members.name: Insert
members.signature: public int Insert(int atIndex, Color value)
members.summary: Inserts a new element at a given position in the array. The position must be valid, or at the end of the array ([code]idx == size()[/code]).

members.name: IsEmpty
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the array is empty.

members.name: PushBack
members.summary: Appends a value to the array.

members.name: RemoveAt
members.summary: Removes an element from the array by index.

members.name: Resize
members.summary: Sets the size of the array. If the array is grown, reserves elements at the end of the array. If the array is shrunk, truncates the array to the new size. Calling [method resize] once and assigning the new values is faster than adding new elements one by one. Returns [constant OK] on success, or one of the following [enum Error] constants if this method fails: [constant ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] if the size is negative, or [constant ERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY] if allocations fail. Use [method size] to find the actual size of the array after resize.

members.name: Reverse
members.summary: Reverses the order of the elements in the array.

members.name: Rfind
members.signature: public int Rfind(Color value, int from)
members.summary: Searches the array in reverse order. Optionally, a start search index can be passed. If negative, the start index is considered relative to the end of the array.

members.name: Set
members.signature: public void Set(int index, Color value)
members.summary: Changes the [Color] at the given index.

members.name: Size
members.summary: Returns the number of elements in the array.

members.name: Slice
members.signature: public PackedColorArray Slice(int begin, int end)
members.summary: Returns the slice of the [PackedColorArray], from [param begin] (inclusive) to [param end] (exclusive), as a new [PackedColorArray]. The absolute value of [param begin] and [param end] will be clamped to the array size, so the default value for [param end] makes it slice to the size of the array by default (i.e. [code]arr.slice(1)[/code] is a shorthand for [code]arr.slice(1, arr.size())[/code]). If either [param begin] or [param end] are negative, they will be relative to the end of the array (i.e. [code]arr.slice(0, -2)[/code] is a shorthand for [code]arr.slice(0, arr.size() - 2)[/code]).

members.name: Sort
members.summary: Sorts the elements of the array in ascending order.

members.name: ToByteArray
members.summary: Returns a [PackedByteArray] with each color encoded as bytes.

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if contents of the arrays differ.

operators.name: operator +
operators.summary: Returns a new [PackedColorArray] with contents of [param right] added at the end of this array. For better performance, consider using [method append_array] instead.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if contents of both arrays are the same, i.e. they have all equal [Color]s at the corresponding indices.

operators.name: operator []
operators.summary: Returns the [Color] at index [param index]. Negative indices can be used to access the elements starting from the end. Using index out of array's bounds will result in an error.
